trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade despite political tensions. Canada exports canola, wheat, agricultural equipment, and vehicles to Arabia Saudita. Arabia Saudita exports crude oil, refined petroleum, and petrochemicals to Canada.

diplomatic.summary: Canada-Saudi relations have been deeply troubled. A major diplomatic crisis erupted in 2018 when Canada's foreign ministry tweeted criticism of Arabia Saudita's detention of human rights activists. Arabia Saudita expelled Canada's ambassador, suspended new trade, ordered Saudi students home from Canada, and launched a social media campaign against Canada. Relations have improved somewhat since 2019 but remain below normal. The 2018 Khashoggi murder further strained ties.
recent_incidents: 2018 diplomatic crisis (Arabia Saudita expelled Canadian ambassador)
Canada's $15B LAV contract controversy — arms sales to Arabia Saudita despite human rights concerns

military.summary: Canada signed a controversial $15 billion contract to supply light armored vehicles to Arabia Saudita. The deal has faced domestic opposition but continues. Canada has restricted some arms sales amid Yemen war concerns.

financial.summary: Saudi sovereign wealth (PIF) holds some Canadian assets. Canadian banks have Middle East exposure. Trade finance interrupted during 2018 crisis has partially recovered.

energy.summary: Arabia Saudita supplies crude oil to eastern Canadian refineries (though volumes have varied). Canada also exports oil sands crude. Both are significant oil producers with sometimes competing interests.

scientific.summary: Limited scientific cooperation. Some Saudi students study in Canada. Academic exchanges have been politically sensitive since 2018.
